---
title: "Interactive Exploratory Data Analysis"
execute:
    echo: false
format:
    html:
        toc: true
        code-overflow: wrap
        embed-resources: true
---

This document contains interactive visualizations for the TTC Bus Delay project. 

```{python}
#| summary: setup
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import requests
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

data_dir = Path("data")
ttc_path = data_dir / "ttc-bus-delay-data-2024.csv"
parking_paths = sorted(data_dir.glob("Parking_Tags_Data_2024_*.csv"))

ttc_raw = pd.read_csv(ttc_path)
parking_raw = pd.concat((pd.read_csv(p) for p in parking_paths), ignore_index=True)

ttc_clean = ttc_raw.rename(columns=lambda c: c.strip().lower().replace(" ", "_"))
ttc_clean["date"] = pd.to_datetime(ttc_clean["date"], format="%d-%b-%y", errors="coerce")
ttc_clean["min_delay"] = pd.to_numeric(ttc_clean["min_delay"], errors="coerce")
ttc_clean = ttc_clean.dropna(subset=["date", "min_delay"])
ttc_clean = ttc_clean[ttc_clean["date"].dt.year == 2024].copy()

parking_clean = parking_raw.rename(columns=lambda c: c.strip().lower())
parking_clean["date"] = pd.to_datetime(parking_clean["date_of_infraction"].astype(str), format="%Y%m%d", errors="coerce")
parking_clean["set_fine_amount"] = pd.to_numeric(parking_clean["set_fine_amount"], errors="coerce")
parking_clean = parking_clean.dropna(subset=["date"])
parking_clean = parking_clean[parking_clean["date"].dt.year == 2024].copy()

parking_daily = parking_clean.groupby("date", as_index=False).agg(
    tickets_issued=("date_of_infraction", "size"),
    unique_infraction_types=("infraction_code", "nunique"),
)
parking_daily["tickets_log1p"] = np.log1p(parking_daily["tickets_issued"])

toronto_lat, toronto_lon = 43.6548, -79.3884
weather_url = (
    f"https://archive-api.open-meteo.com/v1/archive?latitude={toronto_lat}&longitude={toronto_lon}"
    "&start_date=2024-01-01&end_date=2024-12-31"
    "&daily=temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_sum,snowfall_sum,windspeed_10m_max&timezone=America%2FToronto"
)
weather_json = requests.get(weather_url, timeout=30).json()
weather_daily = pd.DataFrame(weather_json["daily"]).rename(columns={"time": "date"})
weather_daily["date"] = pd.to_datetime(weather_daily["date"], errors="coerce")
weather_cols = ["temperature_2m_max", "temperature_2m_min", "precipitation_sum", "snowfall_sum", "windspeed_10m_max"]
for col in weather_cols:
    weather_daily[col] = pd.to_numeric(weather_daily[col], errors="coerce")

route_day_df = ttc_clean.groupby(["date", "route"], as_index=False).agg(
    total_delay_mins=("min_delay", "sum"),
    delay_events=("min_delay", "count")
)

# Merge weather and parking proxy onto the route-day base
model_df = (
    route_day_df
    .merge(weather_daily, on="date", how="left")
    .merge(parking_daily, on="date", how="left")
).dropna()
```

## Interactive Route-Day Heatmap

This interactive heatmap allows us to visualize the density of delays across different bus routes over time. You can toggle between viewing the total minutes of delay and the sheer count of delay events.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

# Filter to top 30 routes for readability in the heatmap
top_routes = model_df.groupby("route")["total_delay_mins"].sum().nlargest(30).index
heatmap_df = model_df[model_df["route"].isin(top_routes)].copy()

heatmap_df["route"] = heatmap_df["route"].astype(str)

fig1 = px.density_heatmap(
    heatmap_df,
    x="date",
    y="route",
    z="total_delay_mins",
    histfunc="sum",
    color_continuous_scale="Viridis",
    title="Top 30 Routes: Daily Delay Burden Heatmap",
    labels={"date": "Date", "route": "Bus Route", "total_delay_mins": "Total Delay (mins)"}
)

fig1.update_layout(
    updatemenus=[
        dict(
            type="buttons",
            direction="right",
            x=1.0,
            y=1.15,
            showactive=True,
            buttons=list([
                dict(
                    label="Total Delay Minutes",
                    method="update",
                    args=[{"z": [heatmap_df["total_delay_mins"]]},
                          {"title": "Top 30 Routes: Daily Delay Minutes Heatmap",
                           "coloraxis.colorbar.title": "Minutes"}]
                ),
                dict(
                    label="Delay Event Count",
                    method="update",
                    args=[{"z": [heatmap_df["delay_events"]]},
                          {"title": "Top 30 Routes: Daily Delay Event Count Heatmap",
                           "coloraxis.colorbar.title": "Events"}]
                )
            ]),
        )
    ]
)
fig1.show()
```

## Route Ranking Explorer

This visualization dynamically ranks the TTC bus routes based on their cumulative delay metrics. Use the slider to filter the number of top routes displayed, and the dropdown to toggle between total minutes lost and the number of distinct delay events.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

# Aggregate route totals
route_agg = model_df.groupby("route", as_index=False).agg(
    total_mins=("total_delay_mins", "sum"),
    total_events=("delay_events", "sum")
)

# Sort by minutes for initial view
route_agg = route_agg.sort_values("total_mins", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
route_agg["route"] = route_agg["route"].astype(str)

fig2 = go.Figure()

fig2.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=route_agg["route"],
        y=route_agg["total_mins"],
        marker_color="#4c78a8",
        name="Total Minutes"
    )
)

fig2.update_layout(
    title="Interactive Route Delay Rankings",
    xaxis_title="Bus Route",
    yaxis_title="Metric Value",
    updatemenus=[
        dict(
            type="dropdown",
            direction="down",
            x=0.1,
            y=1.15,
            showactive=True,
            buttons=list([
                dict(
                    label="Total Delay Minutes",
                    method="update",
                    args=[{"y": [route_agg["total_mins"]]}, {"yaxis.title.text": "Total Delay (mins)"}]
                ),
                dict(
                    label="Total Delay Events",
                    method="update",
                    args=[{"y": [route_agg["total_events"]]}, {"yaxis.title.text": "Total Events"}]
                )
            ]),
        )
    ]
)

# Create slider steps
steps = []
for i in range(10, min(100, len(route_agg)), 10):
    step = dict(
        method="update",
        args=[{"x": [route_agg["route"].head(i)], "y": [route_agg["total_mins"].head(i)]}],
        label=str(i)
    )
    steps.append(step)

sliders = [dict(
    active=0,
    currentvalue={"prefix": "Show Top N Routes: "},
    pad={"t": 50},
    steps=steps
)]

fig2.update_layout(
    sliders=sliders,
    xaxis_tickangle=-45
)

# Initialize with top 10
fig2.update_traces(x=route_agg["route"].head(10), y=route_agg["total_mins"].head(10))

fig2.show()
```

## Weather vs. Delay Scatterplot Explorer

To understand how environmental conditions impact bus operations, this scatterplot compares daily delays against dynamic weather variables. You can toggle the X-axis parameter to explore the specific relationships between extreme delays and temperature, precipitation, snowfall, or windspeed.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

daily_weather_impact = model_df.groupby("date", as_index=False).agg(
    system_delay_mins=("total_delay_mins", "sum"),
    system_delay_events=("delay_events", "sum"),
    temperature_2m_max=("temperature_2m_max", "max"),
    precipitation_sum=("precipitation_sum", "max"),
    snowfall_sum=("snowfall_sum", "max"),
    windspeed_10m_max=("windspeed_10m_max", "max")
)

fig3 = go.Figure()

fig3.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=daily_weather_impact["temperature_2m_max"],
        y=daily_weather_impact["system_delay_mins"],
        mode="markers",
        marker=dict(
            size=10,
            color=daily_weather_impact["system_delay_mins"],
            colorscale="Inferno",
            showscale=True,
            colorbar=dict(title="System Delay (Mins)")
        ),
        text=daily_weather_impact["date"].astype(str),
        hovertemplate="Date: %{text}<br>Weather: %{x}<br>System Delay: %{y} mins<extra></extra>"
    )
)

fig3.update_layout(
    title="System-wide Delay vs. Weather Conditions",
    xaxis_title="Max Temperature (°C)",
    yaxis_title="Total System Delay (minutes)",
    updatemenus=[
        dict(
            active=0,
            buttons=list([
                dict(label="Max Temperature (°C)",
                     method="update",
                     args=[{"x": [daily_weather_impact["temperature_2m_max"]]},
                           {"xaxis": {"title": "Max Temperature (°C)"}}]),
                dict(label="Precipitation (mm)",
                     method="update",
                     args=[{"x": [daily_weather_impact["precipitation_sum"]]},
                           {"xaxis": {"title": "Precipitation (mm)"}}]),
                dict(label="Snowfall (cm)",
                     method="update",
                     args=[{"x": [daily_weather_impact["snowfall_sum"]]},
                           {"xaxis": {"title": "Snowfall (cm)"}}]),
                dict(label="Windspeed (km/h)",
                     method="update",
                     args=[{"x": [daily_weather_impact["windspeed_10m_max"]]},
                           {"xaxis": {"title": "Windspeed (km/h)"}}]),
            ]),
            direction="down",
            pad={"r": 10, "t": 10},
            showactive=True,
            x=0.2,
            xanchor="left",
            y=1.15,
            yanchor="top"
        ),
    ]
)

fig3.show()
```
